title: NA-ULIP
word_text:
1 Lakh Investment Plan
If you’ve saved up ₹1 lakh, the right thing you can do is make that money work for you. Instead of letting it sit idle, you can put it in various investment plans that suit your goals and risk tolerance. Whether you want steady growth, higher returns, or a balance of both, there are several ways to put this amount to good use. Let us cover some one lakh investment plan options that can work as a solid starting point to build long-term wealth and achieve your financial milestones.
What is a ₹1 Lakh Investment Plan?
A ₹1 lakh investment plan is basically a clear strategy for how you want to use your ₹1 lakh to grow your money. Based on your purpose, the time period you can stay invested, and the estimated returns, you can choose from various financial options. Your choice also depends on how much risk you’re comfortable taking:
Low-risk options offer steady, predictable returns and suit goals like building an emergency fund or planning short-term expenses. Investment options are FDs, PPF, and Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana.
Medium-risk options balance growth and stability, making them useful for medium-term goals such as a car purchase or children’s education planning. Examples include debt funds and government or corporate bonds.
High-risk options can be volatile but offer higher long-term growth, ideal for goals like wealth creation or retirement planning. You can invest in mutual funds or ULIPs.
You can choose to invest the entire ₹1 lakh at once or divide it into smaller parts. For example, you could invest ₹8,000 every month until the full amount is invested. This makes investing feel easier and more manageable.
Best Investment Options for ₹1 Lakh
Here are some of the best investment plans for growing your ₹1 lakh:
Life Insurance Plans
Life insurance plans, like endowment plans, money back plans, ULIPs, etc., can provide insurance coverage with the added benefit of savings over the policy tenure. Here are some of the salient features of these plans –
Endowment plans – endowment plans combine insurance coverage and savings creation in one. You can choose with-profit endowment plans for added bonus additions.
Money-back plans – Money-back plans are like endowment plans, but with the added benefit of regular income. These plans pay back benefits during the policy tenure that can help you supplement your income and meet important financial obligations.
ULIPs – ULIPs help you enjoy insurance protection and market-linked returns under a single plan. A part of the premium that you pay is used to provide life insurance coverage, while the remaining is allocated to market-linked funds.
Child Investment Plans
If you want to save for your child’s higher education or marriage, you can choose child investment plans. Some of the common examples include life insurance plans like endowment plans, money-back plans, or ULIPs, Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana, fixed deposits or recurring deposits, etc.
Fixed Deposit
One of the safest and simplest ways to invest ₹1 lakh is via a fixed deposit. You can invest for a minimum of 7 days to 10 years to get a predictable return at the end of the selected tenure. The interest rate and the returns are guaranteed. You can use an online FD calculator to know the maturity sum.
Recurring Deposit
A Recurring Deposit (RD) is another easy, disciplined way to grow your ₹1 lakh over time. You can invest your savings in a systematic manner. Your deposits earn interest at a fixed rate, compounded quarterly, and you receive the total amount (principal + interest) when the RD matures. Since the returns are guaranteed and not linked to the market, RDs are ideal for steady savers who want predictable growth. You can use an RD calculator to determine the best return on investment by adjusting the parameters: monthly amount, tenure, and interest rate.
Money Market Account
(Not sure if this is available in India)
Corporate Fixed Deposits
Corporate FDs let you invest your ₹1 lakh with NBFCs or financial companies for a fixed period at a fixed interest rate, which is often higher than regular bank FDs. Tenures can range from a few months to a few years, making them useful for short-term goals, such as planning an international trip or buying a special gift. You can choose deposits with strong credit ratings, such as AAA, as lower ratings indicate higher risk.
Mutual Funds
Investing ₹1 lakh in mutual funds is a flexible way to grow your money while spreading the risk. You can invest the full amount at once or start an SIP (Systematic Investment Plan). Here are the three main fund types:
Equity funds: Invest mainly in stocks, best for a long-term investment plan, but come with higher market risk.
Debt funds: Invest in safer instruments like bonds; ideal for medium-term goals with moderate, stable returns.
Hybrid funds: Mix equity and debt, giving you growth plus stability in one fund.
Stocks
The stock market lets you directly buy shares of publicly listed companies and benefit from their growth. To start your one lakh investment plan, you need to open a Demat account and link it to your bank account for smooth buying and selling of shares. Before choosing any stock, be clear about your financial goals and how long you want to stay invested. Also consider your risk tolerance.
Government Schemes
These government-backed options are low-risk and ideal for adding stability and tax benefits to your ₹1 lakh investment plan.
Key Points to Consider When Investing 1 Lakh
Before deciding where to invest, it is helpful to consider a few simple but important factors that guide you toward the right choice:
Financial milestones:
Be clear about what you’re saving for: car, home, your child’s education, or retirement. Your goal decides the type of investment you should pick.
Risk appetite:
Understand how much risk you’re comfortable taking. Higher risk allows you to explore market-linked options, while lower risk points you toward safer, government-backed choices.
Performance:
Check how the investment has performed in the past and compare it with similar alternatives, especially if you’re choosing market-linked avenues.
Lock-in tenure:
Some options come with a fixed lock-in period, meaning you can’t withdraw before a certain time. Ensure the timeline works for your needs.
Expenses:
Investment schemes have some charges, such as expense ratios, exit loads, or brokerage fees, that can reduce returns, so factor these in to calculate the return on investment.
Tax benefits:
Check the tax implications too, so that you can save taxes on investments and also on the returns earned.
Key Takeaways
Invest ₹1 lakh in different schemes based on your financial objective, tenure of investment, returns expected, and risk appetite.
There are different types of investment options. Some fall in the high-risk category, some have moderate risk profiles, while others are low-risk.
If you want stable returns, you can choose fixed or recurring deposits, PPF, life insurance endowment plans, etc.
For market-linked returns, you can consider ULIPs, mutual funds, stocks, etc.
To start your ₹1 lakh investment plan, assess your risk profile, goals, performance and lock-in tenure of the investment scheme, and tax benefits available.
Conclusion
Your ₹1 lakh investment plan can become the foundation for creating a corpus for your goals. Consider what truly aligns with your goals, comfort with risk, and the time you’re willing to stay invested. Whether you prefer safe options like life insurance, endowment plans or FDs, or want growth through ULIP, mutual funds, or other market-linked avenues, research the options before investing. A good investment plan isn’t something you set once and forget. It needs regular check-ins so you can adjust it as your goals, income, or market conditions change.
Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)
What are the ways in which I can diversify a ₹1 lakh portfolio?
You can split your ₹1 lakh across different asset types to balance risk and returns. You can choose a mix of equity, debt and other asset classes to mitigate risks and enhance the return potential of your portfolio.
What will be the best choice for investing ₹1 lakh?
There’s no single “best” choice for everyone. The choice is based on your investment period, goals, and risk appetite. If you want safety, options such as fixed or recurring deposits, life insurance endowment plans, etc., are good choices. If you want market-linked returns, you can explore options such as ULIPs, stocks, and mutual funds.
What returns can I get on a ₹1 lakh investment for a period of six months?
Returns over six months vary by investment type. Some investment avenues, like FDs, RDs, corporate deposits, etc., give guaranteed returns. However, if you have selected market-linked avenues, the returns would depend on market performance and cannot be predicted.
How to invest 1 lakh and get a monthly income?
There are various options to choose from: Post Office Monthly Income Scheme (POMIS), Senior Citizen Savings Scheme (SCSS), Systematic Withdrawal Plans (SWPs), fixed deposits that give monthly interest income, etc. that you can select depending on your risk appetite and financial goals.
Disclaimer
IN THIS POLICY, THE INVESTMENT RISK IN INVESTMENT PORTFOLIO IS BORNE BY THE POLICYHOLDER
The Unit Linked Insurance Products do not offer any liquidity during the first five years of the contract. The policyholder will not be able to surrender or withdraw the monies invested in Unit Linked Insurance Products completely or partially till the end of the fifth year.
ULIPs are different from the traditional insurance products and are subject to the risk factors. The premium paid in ULIPs are subject to investment risks associated with capital markets and the NAVs of the units may go up or down based on the performance of fund and factors influencing the capital market and the insured is responsible for his/her decisions. Please know the associated risks and the applicable charges, from your Insurance agent or the Intermediary or policy document issued by the insurance company. The various funds offered under this contract are the names of the funds and do not in any way indicate the quality of these plans, their future prospects and returns.
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajlifeinsurance.com) carefully before concluding a sale. full_pdf_text:
1 Lakh Investment Plan
If you’ve saved up ₹1 lakh, the right thing you can do is make that money work for you. Instead of letting it sit idle, you can put it in various financial plans that suit your goals and risk tolerance. Whether you want steady growth, higher returns, or a balance of both, there are several ways to put this amount to good use. Let us cover some one lakh investment plan options that can work as a solid starting point to build long-term wealth and achieve your financial milestones.
What is a ₹1 Lakh Investment Plan?
A ₹1 lakh investment plan is basically a structured approach to managing a lump sum of ₹1 lakhs in a way that balances growth, safety, and financial goals. Based on your purpose, the time period you can stay invested, and the estimated returns, you can choose from various financial options. Your choice also depends on how much risk you’re comfortable taking:
1. Low-risk options offer steady, predictable returns and suit goals like building an emergency
fund or planning short-term expenses. Investment options are FDs, PPF, Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana etc. 2. Medium-risk options balance growth and stability, making them useful for medium-term
goals such as a car purchase or children’s education planning. Examples include debt funds and government or corporate bonds. 3. High-risk options can be volatile but offer higher long-term growth, ideal for goals like
wealth creation or retirement planning.
You can choose to invest the entire ₹1 lakh at once or divide it into smaller parts. For example, you could invest ₹8,000 every month until the full amount is invested. This makes investing feel easier and more manageable.
Investment Options for ₹1 Lakh
Here are some few plans you can explore to make the most of your ₹1 lakh:
1. Life Insurance Plans
Life insurance plans, like endowment plans, money back plans, ULIPs, etc., can provide life insurance coverage with the added benefit of savings over the policy tenure. Here are some of the salient features of these plans –
o Endowment plans – endowment plans combine life insurance coverage and savings
creation in one. You can choose PAR endowment plans for added bonus (if declared) additions.# o Money-back plans – Money‑back plans provide life cover along with periodic
payouts at predefined intervals known as survival benefit , giving you regular cash flow during the policy term while still offering a maturity benefit at the end. These plans can help you supplement your income and meet important financial obligations.
o ULIPs – ULIPs help you enjoy life insurance protection and market-linked returns
under a single plan. A part of the premium that you pay is used to provide life insurance coverage, while the remaining is allocated to market-linked funds. 2. Child Based Plans
If you want to save for your child’s higher education or marriage, you can choose child based plans. Some of the common examples include life insurance plans like endowment plans, money-back plans, or ULIPs, Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana, fixed deposits or recurring deposits, etc. 3. Fixed Deposit1
Fixed deposits allow you to invest for a minimum period of 7 days up to a maximum of 10 years and get a predictable return at the end of the selected tenure. The interest rate and the returns are guaranteed. You can use an online FD calculator to know the maturity sum. 4. Recurring Deposit
A Recurring Deposit (RD) is another easy, disciplined way to grow your ₹1 lakh over time. Your deposits earn interest at a fixed rate, compounded quarterly, and you receive the total amount (principal + interest) when the RD matures. Since the returns are guaranteed and not linked to the market, RDs are considered by steady savers who want predictable growth. You can use an RD calculator to determine the best return on investment by adjusting the parameters: monthly amount, tenure, and interest rate.
5. Corporate Fixed Deposits
Corporate FDs let you invest your ₹1 lakh with NBFCs or financial companies for a fixed period at a fixed interest rate. Tenures can range from a few months to a few years, making them useful for short-term goals, such as planning an international trip or buying a special gift. You can choose deposits with strong credit ratings, such as AAA, as lower ratings indicate higher risk of default on interest or principal payments. 6. Mutual Funds
Mutual funds are a flexible way to grow your money while spreading the risk. The entire amount can be invested at once or periodically as SIP (Systematic Investment Plan). Here are the three main fund types:
● Equity funds: These funds invest mainly in stocks, and are considered suitable for long-term growth , but are subject to higher market risk.
● Debt funds: These funds invest in safer instruments like bonds; considered suitable for medium-term goals with moderate, stable returns.
● Hybrid funds: These funds invest in a combination of equity and debt, offering a balanced mix of growth (from equities) and stability or income (from debt). 7. Stocks
The stock market lets you directly purchase shares of publicly listed companies and benefit from their potential growth. You need to open a Demat account and link it to your bank account for smooth transactions while buying and selling shares. Before choosing any stock, be clear about your financial goals and how long you want to stay invested. Also consider your risk tolerance. 8. Government Schemes
These government-backed options are low-risk and considered suitable for adding stability and tax benefits to your ₹1 lakh investment plan.
Scheme What It Does
National Pension System (NPS)
A long-term investment plan where your money is invested across equity, debts, bonds, and so on helping you build a retirement corpus with tax benefits.
Public Provident Fund (PPF)
A long-term savings plan with guaranteed returns and tax-free maturity proceeds.
National Savings Certificate (NSC)
A safe, fixed-income option with a 5-year lock-in that offers guaranteed returns.1
Sovereign Gold Bonds (SGBs)
Let you hold gold digitally without dealing with physical storage ; returns come from gold price movements plus annual interest.
Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana (SSY)
Long-term plan that offers attractive interest income and tax advantages, for parents with a girl child. You can create a corpus for your child’s education or marriage goals.
Kisan Vikas Patra (KVP)
A simple fixed return scheme that effectively doubles your investment over a set period.
Senior Citizens Savings Scheme (SCSS)
For senior citizens who are 60 years or above and want to receive a fixed, steady interest income.
Key Points to Consider When Investing 1 Lakh
Before deciding where to invest, it is helpful to consider a few simple but important factors that guide you toward the right choice:
1. Financial milestones:
Be clear about what you’re saving for: car, home, your child’s education, or retirement. Your goal decides the type of investment you should pick. 2. Risk appetite:
Understand how much risk you’re comfortable taking. Higher risk allows you to explore market-linked options, while lower risk points you toward safer, government-backed choices. 3. Performance:
Check how the investment has performed in the past and compare it with similar alternatives, especially if you’re choosing market-linked avenues.* 4. Lock-in tenure:
Some options come with a fixed lock-in period, meaning you can’t withdraw before a certain time. Ensure the timeline works for your needs. 5. Expenses:
Investment schemes have some charges, such as expense ratios, exit loads, or brokerage fees, that can reduce returns, so factor these in to calculate the return on investment. 6. Tax benefits:
Check the tax implications too, so that you can save taxes on investments and also on the returns earned.
Key Takeaways
1. Invest ₹1 lakh in different schemes based on your financial objective, tenure of investment,
returns expected, and risk appetite. 2. There are different types of investment options. Some fall in the high-risk category, some
have moderate risk profiles, while others are low-risk. 3. If you want stable returns, you can choose life insurance plans such as endowment plans
fixed or recurring deposits, PPF, etc. 4. For market-linked returns, you can consider ULIPs, mutual funds, stocks, etc. 5. To start your ₹1 lakh investment plan, assess your risk profile, goals, performance and lock-
in tenure of the investment scheme, and tax benefits available.
Conclusion
Your ₹1 lakh investment plan can become the foundation for creating a corpus for your goals. Consider what truly aligns with your goals, comfort with risk, and the time you’re willing to stay invested. Whether you prefer safe options like life insurance, or FDs, , mutual funds, or other market-linked avenues, research the options before investing. A good investment plan isn’t something you set once and forget. It needs regular check-ins so you can adjust it as your goals, income, or market conditions change.
Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)
1. What are the ways in which I can diversify a ₹1 lakh portfolio?
You can split your ₹1 lakh across different asset types to balance risk and returns. You can choose a mix of asset classes to mitigate risks and enhance the return potential of your portfolio. 2. What will be the best choice for investing ₹1 lakh?
There’s no single “best” choice for everyone. The choice is based on your investment period, goals, and risk appetite. If you want safety, options such as life insurance plans like endowment plans, fixed or recurring deposits, etc., are good choices. If you want market- linked returns, you can explore options such as life insurance plans like ULIPs, stocks, and mutual funds. 3. What returns can I get on a ₹1 lakh investment for a period of six months?
Returns over six months vary by investment type. Some investment avenues, like FDs, RDs, corporate deposits, etc., give guaranteed returns. However, if you have selected market- linked avenues, the returns would depend on market performance and cannot be predicted. 4. How can I use 1 lakh and get a monthly income?
There are various options to choose from: Post Office Monthly Income Scheme (POMIS), Systematic Withdrawal Plans (SWPs), fixed deposits that give monthly interest income, etc. that you can select depending on your risk appetite and financial goals.
Disclaimer

ULIPs are different from the traditional insurance products and are subject to the risk factors. The premium paid in ULIPs are subject to investment risks associated with capital markets and the NAVs of the units may go up or down based on the performance of fund and factors influencing the capital market and the insured is responsible for his/her decisions. Please know the associated risks and the applicable charges, from your Insurance agent or the Intermediary or policy document issued by the insurance company. The various funds offered under this contract are the names of the funds and do not in any way indicate the quality of these plans, their future prospects and returns.
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. changes.pdf_final: These government-backed options are low-risk and considered suitable for adding stability and tax benefits to your ₹1 lakh investment plan.
Scheme What It Does
National Pension System (NPS)
A long-term investment plan where your money is invested across equity, debts, bonds, and so on helping you build a retirement corpus with tax benefits.
Public Provident Fund (PPF)
A long-term savings plan with guaranteed returns and tax-free maturity proceeds.
National Savings Certificate (NSC)
A safe, fixed-income option with a 5-year lock-in that offers guaranteed returns.1
Sovereign Gold Bonds (SGBs)
Let you hold gold digitally without dealing with physical storage ; returns come from gold price movements plus annual interest.
Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana (SSY)
Long-term plan that offers attractive interest income and tax advantages, for parents with a girl child.
You can create a corpus for your child’s education or marriage goals.
Kisan Vikas Patra (KVP)
A simple fixed return scheme that effectively doubles your investment over a set period.
Senior Citizens Savings Scheme (SCSS)
For senior citizens who are 60 years or above and want to receive a fixed, steady interest income.
